channel.title: The Brussels Times
channel.description: Latest Brussels posts from The Brussels Times.

item.title: In Brussels, single mothers struggle to remain in the job market
item.description: In Brussels, one in three households with children (33.7%) are single-parent families.

item.title: Zombieing: The new dating trend no one asked for
item.description: I was in my Bridget Jones era and feeling unusually satisfied with singlehood. But then I got an unexpected text.

item.title: Agreement in place to represent all sensitivities of Islam in worship
item.description: A landmark meeting in Brussels brings hope for unity as Belgium’s Islamic communities move to end years of organisational turmoil.

item.title: Ixelles cracks down on self-service fast-food joints by raising taxes
item.description: "We consider that within a commercial fabric, it diminishes the more convivial aspect," an Ixelles councillor told The Brussels Times.

item.title: New edition of Brussels zine celebrates Wiels Quarter and its 'Unionistes' fan bars
item.description: "This is what the Pintjes zine is all about: landing in an area of the city you don’t know, wanting to go for a beer but not knowing where to start."

item.title: Air pollution in Brussels: Call for 'green school streets' gets louder as harmful fumes persist
item.description: More than 120 schools in the Brussels-Capital Region are exposed to pollution levels between two and three times above the WHO threshold.

item.title: 'Brussels is afraid to be ambitious': Kanal museum’s quitting director has no regrets
item.description: "It’s not easy to fulfil such a project of this scale in Brussels. It will be the biggest museum to open in Brussels in more than a century."

item.title: 'We want the city centre to be spotless': Steam-cleaning operation launched to clean Brussels street furniture
item.description: Benches, traffic signs, smart bins, public toilets, lighting poles, fountains, bicycle racks and kiosks are all being cleaned with superheated water and steam.

item.title: Summer Dance Festival returns to Bozar
item.description: Around 15 artists will perform in the Henry le Bœuf Hall, including Sangeun Lee and Gareth Haw, principal dancers of the English National Ballet.

item.title: Francophone teachers protest austerity plans in Brussels
item.description: Hundreds of French-speaking educators took to the streets on Friday to send a clear message to the government as budget cuts loom for schools.

item.title: New method reveals Brussels' missing cycling paths
item.description: Over 30 critical Brussels locations lack cycling infrastructure despite traffic levels matching the Region's busiest bike routes.

item.title: Brussels motorway entrance becomes green boulevard with skate rink
item.description: Avenue des Croix du Feu in the Brussels district of Neder-over-Heembeek has been completely overhauled to make room for cyclists, nature, and recreation.

item.title: Power outage shuts down Brussels' Palace of Justice
item.description: Several dozen people were waiting outside the palace around 09:30, in front of closed doors.

item.title: What to do in Brussels this weekend: 22 - 24 May
item.description: From Shakespeare and Cervantes to outdoor jazz across the city centre, here's what's on in Brussels this weekend.

item.title: 'I paid €49 for speed dating in Brussels, and it was disastrous'
item.description: One Brussels resident recounts her "disastrous" experience of speed dating in the city – which has put her off from ever going again.

item.title: 'Why scrap this? We want to work': The Brussels cleaners under threat from new funding reforms
item.description: Companies in the domestic services sector have long been able to receive funding for social employment integration, but as of next year this will change – prompting fears of lay-offs.

item.title: Nearly 4 out of 10 Belgians struggle to make ends meet
item.description: Fewer Belgians are struggling to make ends meet, but disparities remain striking, according to the Belgian statistical office Statbel.

item.title: Partial solar eclipse to be visible in Belgium this summer
item.description: The sun will be approximately 90% covered during the eclipse.

item.title: Fuel spills on public road in Schaerbeek
item.description: The Brussels fire brigade has been cleaning the road in Schaerbeek, after a trunk have spilled fuel in the morning.

item.title: Man on the run after the shooting of three bystanders as they left a cafe in Forest
item.description: Several individuals, armed with an Uzi submachine gun and a Kalashnikov automatic rifle, opened fire in a residential square in Forest in 2024.
